problem_statement.title: Fix non-equi join error with equi + 2 inequality conditions (#7641)
problem_statement.body:
## Summary

  Fixes #7641. Non-equi joins combining an equality condition with two inequality conditions on the same column (e.g., `on = .(id == id, val >= lo, val <= hi)`) error on
  1.18.0+ due to `rightcols` being overwritten by `chmatchdup`.

  When `rightcols` has duplicates (same `x` column in multiple conditions), `chmatchdup` remaps them into the expanded result namespace `nx`. This overwrote `rightcols`,
  causing downstream code (`.shallow(x, rightcols)` in `.join_result_key`) to reference columns beyond `ncol(x)`.

  **Fix:** Introduce `ansrightcols` for the remapped indices, keep `rightcols` as original column indices into `x`. Only `icolsAns` needs the remapped values; all other
  downstream uses need the original x-relative indices.

  ## Changes

  - `R/data.table.R`: Replace `rightcols = chmatchdup(...)` with `ansrightcols = chmatchdup(...)`, use `ansrightcols` only for `icolsAns`
  - `inst/tests/tests.Rraw`: Regression test (1483.91)
  - `NEWS.md`: Bug fix entry
problem_statement.issue_title: Non-equi join with equi + 2 inequality conditions errors on 1.18.0+ (worked on 1.17.x)
problem_statement.issue_body:
Non-equi join with equi + 2 inequality conditions errors on 1.18.0+ (worked on 1.17.x)

## Minimal reproducible example

```r
library(data.table)

x <- data.table(id = "A", val = 1:10, price = 1:10 * 10)
setkey(x, id, val)
i <- data.table(id = "A", lo = 4L, hi = 6L)

# equi + 2 non-equi: FAILS
x[i, on = .(id, val >= lo, val <= hi), nomatch = NA, verbose = TRUE]
#> Error in colnamesInt(x, cols, check_dups = FALSE) :
#>   argument specifying columns received non-existing column(s): cols[3]=4

# 2 non-equi only (no equi part): works
x2 <- data.table(val = 1:10, price = 1:10 * 10)
i2 <- data.table(lo = 4L, hi = 6L)
x2[i2, on = .(val >= lo, val <= hi), nomatch = NA]
#> 3 rows, works

# equi + 1 non-equi: works
i3 <- data.table(id = "A", lo = 4L)
x[i3, on = .(id, val >= lo), nomatch = NA]
#> 7 rows, works
```

The error is in `.join_result_key()` → `.shallow(x, rightcols)` → `colnamesInt()`. It computes a spurious column index of `ncol(x) + 1`:

| `ncol(x)` | Error |
|-----------|-------|
| 3 | `cols[3]=4` |
| 4 | `cols[4]=5` |
| 5 | `cols[3]=6` |

Any non-equi join combining equi conditions with **two** inequality conditions fails. Fewer inequality conditions or removing the equi part works.

## Version bisection

| Version | Result |
|---------|--------|
| 1.16.4 | Works |
| 1.17.0 | Works |
| **1.18.0** | **Fails** |
| 1.18.2.1 | Fails |
| 1.18.99 (dev) | Fails |

**Note:** I tested on R 4.3.3. 
## `sessionInfo()`

```
R version 4.3.3 (2024-02-29)
Platform: x86_64-pc-linux-gnu (64-bit)
Running under: Ubuntu 24.04.1 LTS

Matrix products: default
BLAS:   /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/blas/libblas.so.3.12.0
LAPACK: /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/lapack/liblapack.so.3.12.0

locale:
 [1] LC_CTYPE=en_US.UTF-8       LC_NUMERIC=C
 [3] LC_TIME=en_US.UTF-8        LC_COLLATE=en_US.UTF-8
 [5] LC_MONETARY=en_US.UTF-8    LC_MESSAGES=en_US.UTF-8
 [7] LC_PAPER=en_US.UTF-8       LC_NAME=C
 [9] LC_ADDRESS=C               LC_TELEPHONE=C
[11] LC_MEASUREMENT=en_US.UTF-8 LC_IDENTIFICATION=C

data.table 1.18.2.1
```

patch:
diff --git a/DESCRIPTION b/DESCRIPTION
index 3860e0c48..5998b3279 100644
--- a/DESCRIPTION
+++ b/DESCRIPTION
@@ -107,5 +107,6 @@ Authors@R: c(
   person("Vinit", "Thakur",        role="ctb"),
   person("Mukul", "Kumar",         role="ctb"),
   person("Ildikó", "Czeller",      role="ctb"),
-  person("Manmita", "Das",         role="ctb")
+  person("Manmita", "Das",         role="ctb"),
+  person("Tarun", "Thammisetty",   role="ctb")
   )
diff --git a/NEWS.md b/NEWS.md
index fd0ee8bf1..7cf63f0b7 100644
--- a/NEWS.md
+++ b/NEWS.md
@@ -38,6 +38,8 @@
 
 4. `rowwiseDT()` now provides a helpful error message when a complex object that is not a list (e.g., a function) is provided as a cell value, instructing the user to wrap it in `list()`, [#7219](https://github.com/Rdatatable/data.table/issues/7219). Thanks @kylebutts for the report and @venom1204 for the fix.
 
+5. Non-equi joins combining an equality condition with two inequality conditions on the same column (e.g., `on = .(id == id, val >= lo, val <= hi)`) no longer error, [#7641](https://github.com/Rdatatable/data.table/issues/7641). The internal `chmatchdup` remapping of duplicate `rightcols` was overwriting the original column indices, causing downstream code to reference non-existent columns. Thanks @tarun-t for the report and fix, and @aitap for the diagnosis.
+
 ### Notes
 
 1. {data.table} now depends on R 3.5.0 (2018).
diff --git a/R/data.table.R b/R/data.table.R
index 85d623d39..a989538b1 100644
--- a/R/data.table.R
+++ b/R/data.table.R
@@ -1075,12 +1075,15 @@ replace_dot_alias = function(e) {
       if (length(tt)) jisvars[tt] = paste0("i.",jisvars[tt])
       if (length(duprightcols <- rightcols[duplicated(rightcols)])) {
         nx = c(names_x, names_x[duprightcols])
-        rightcols = chmatchdup(names_x[rightcols], nx)
+        ansrightcols = chmatchdup(names_x[rightcols], nx) # indices into result namespace nx, #7641
         nx = make.unique(nx)
-      } else nx = names_x
+      } else {
+        nx = names_x
+        ansrightcols = rightcols
+      }
       ansvars = make.unique(c(nx, jisvars))
       icols = c(leftcols, seq_along(i)[-leftcols])
-      icolsAns = c(rightcols, seq.int(length(nx)+1L, length.out=ncol(i)-length(unique(leftcols))))
+      icolsAns = c(ansrightcols, seq.int(length(nx)+1L, length.out=ncol(i)-length(unique(leftcols))))
       xcols = xcolsAns = seq_along(x)[-rightcols]
     }
     ansvals = chmatch(ansvars, nx)
test_patch:
diff --git a/inst/tests/tests.Rraw b/inst/tests/tests.Rraw
index f30467dae..508bf6aa0 100644
--- a/inst/tests/tests.Rraw
+++ b/inst/tests/tests.Rraw
@@ -6954,6 +6954,11 @@ DT1 <- data.table(a=1L, key='a')
 DT2 <- data.table(a=2.0, key='a')
 test(1483.83, DT1[DT2, roll='nearest'], data.table(a=2L, key='a'))
 
+# non-equi join with equi + 2 inequality conditions should not error, #7641
+dt_x = data.table(id = c("A","A","B","B"), val = c(1,5,2,4), key = c("id","val"))
+dt_i = data.table(id = c("A","B"), lo = c(2,1), hi = c(6,3))
+test(1483.91, nrow(dt_x[dt_i, on = .(id == id, val >= lo, val <= hi)]), 2L)
+
 # NULL items should be removed when making data.table from list, #842
 # Original fix for #842 added a branch in as.data.table.list() using point()
 # Then PR#3471 moved logic from data.table() into as.data.table.list() and now removes NULL items up front, so longer need for the branch

fail_to_pass: Non-equi join with equi + 2 inequality conditions: `x[i, on = .(id == id, val >= lo, val <= hi)]` should return 2 rows without error
The join should correctly match rows where val is between lo and hi for matching ids
pass_to_pass: Non-equi join with 2 inequality conditions only (no equi part) should continue to work
Non-equi join with equi + 1 inequality condition should continue to work
Standard equi joins should continue to work

key_changes: Initialize `ansrightcols = rightcols` in the else branch when there are no duplicates
Keep `rightcols` unchanged for downstream uses that need original x-relative indices
potential_pitfalls: Forgetting to set `ansrightcols = rightcols` in the else branch when there are no duplicate rightcols
Using `ansrightcols` in places that still need the original column indices
Not understanding the distinction between result namespace indices vs original x column indices

instruction: Fix the non-equi join error that occurs when combining an equality condition with two inequality conditions on the same column (e.g., `on = .(id == id, val >= lo, val <= hi)`). The bug is in the `[.data.table` function where `rightcols` gets overwritten by `chmatchdup` when there are duplicate column references. The fix should introduce a separate variable `ansrightcols` for the remapped indices while keeping `rightcols` as the original column indices into `x`.
context: The bug occurs in R/data.table.R around line 1075-1085 in the join logic. When `rightcols` has duplicates (same `x` column used in multiple conditions like `val >= lo` and `val <= hi`), `chmatchdup` remaps them into the expanded result namespace `nx`. This overwrites `rightcols`, causing downstream code like `.shallow(x, rightcols)` to reference columns beyond `ncol(x)`. The fix requires: (1) creating `ansrightcols` for remapped indices, (2) keeping `rightcols` as original indices, (3) using `ansrightcols` only for `icolsAns`, and (4) handling the else case where there are no duplicates.
